content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 21, 1999


John Barta
Morro Bay Planning Commission
Post Office Box 1558
Morro Bay, California 93443-1558

Re: Your Request for Advice
    Our File No. A-99-190

Dear Mr. Barta:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please keep in mind that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place.² In addition, this letter is solely based upon the facts presented to us in your letter requesting advice, and in the telephone conversation that I had with you on July 6, 1999. The Commission does not act as a finder of fact when issuing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Our advice is applicable only to the extent that the facts provided to us are correct, and that all of the material facts have been provided.

     Furthermore, you should keep in mind that the Commission's jurisdiction is limited to the provisions of the Act. We therefore cannot advise you regarding the impact that other laws, such as Government Code Section 1090³, may have on your situation. We also cannot advise you regarding the applicability of any of your agency's internal policies.

QUESTION

     Does the Act prohibit you from bidding on, or entering into, a contract with the City of Morro Bay, for the provision of design and development services for its web site?


___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Pursuant to regulation 18329, the Commission does not provide advice regarding past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

³ For answers to questions regarding Government Code Section 1090, you should contact the Office of the California Attorney General.

                                                                                                             [illegible]

File No. A-99-190
Page 2

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit you from bidding on, or entering into, a contract with the City
of Morro Bay, for the provision of design and development services for its web site.

FACTS

You are a member of the planning commission for the City of Morro Bay (the "city").
You also own and operate your own business that designs internet web sites.

The city's finance department has recently sent out a lengthy request for proposal (RFP)
to contract with the city for the provision of "design and development services" for the city's
internet web site. The key tasks identified in the RFP are: designing site graphics; testing the
web site for compatibility with all of the most common platforms and monitors; registering the
web site with a variety of search engines; using meta tags to make web site pages visible to
searches; providing appropriate links; training staff to maintain the site; and providing ongoing
technical assistance.

The planning commissioners have no role in deciding which bidder will be awarded the
contract.

You would like to know whether the Act prohibits you, because of your position as a
planning commissioner, from bidding on, or entering into a contract with the city, to provide
design and development services regarding the city's web site.

ANALYSIS

The conflict of interest provisions of the Act do not prohibit a public official from
engaging in any sort of commercial economic activities. As a member of the planning
commission for the City of Morro Bay, you are a public official. (Section 82048.) Nonetheless,
because the conflict of interest provisions of the Act do not regulate the commercial economic
activities of public officials, the Act does not prohibit you from bidding on, or entering into, a
contract with the City of Morro Bay, for the provision of design and development services for its
web site.

The conflict of interest provisions of the Act prohibit a public official from making,
entering into, participating in making, or in any way attempting to use his or her official position to influence a
governmental decision in which the public official knows, or has reason to know, that he or she
has a financial interest. (Section 87100.)

From the facts that you have provided to us, you are not involved in the making of any
governmental decisions about the contract for the provision of design and developmental services
regarding the city's web site. As such, there are no governmental decisions pending that would
give rise to your having a conflict of interest concerning the contract.

File No. A-99-190
Page 3

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Steven Benito Russo
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
